title: Topological flat bands emerging at the inversion of stacking order in rhombohedral graphite
authors: R. Weht, A. A. Aligia, M. Nunez-Regueiro
abstract: Motivated by the indications of high-Tc superconductivity in natural graphite enriched in the rhombohedral phase, we study the band structure of several stacking configurations that combine two of the three graphite structures as well as modifications of the rhombohedral sequence (from ABCABC... to CBACBA...), using first-principles calculations. We focus in particular on the possible emergence of flat bands near the Fermi level. When the two different rhombohedral orderings are combined, flat bands of topological origin emerge at the interface between the two domains, near the K and K' points of the Brillouin zone. Mapping a simple tight-binding model of a rhombohedral slab along the direction perpendicular to the graphene layers onto a Su-Schrieffer-Heeger chain provides a transparent understanding of the underlying physics.
summary: The paper demonstrates that reversing the stacking order in rhombohedral graphite creates a unique interface hosting four topological flat bands. This discovery provides a theoretical mechanism for stable, high-density electronic states that could potentially host superconductivity.
main_problem: Investigating the emergence of topological flat bands at the interface of inverted rhombohedral graphite stacking (ABC to CBA) and their potential link to high-Tc superconductivity.
main_result: The study finds that inverting the rhombohedral stacking order creates four robust topological flat bands at the interface, with charge density concentrated at the inversion layer.
method: First-principles Density Functional Theory (DFT) using VASP and a tight-binding model mapped onto a Su-Schrieffer-Heeger (SSH) chain.
model_or_system: Various graphite stacking configurations, specifically focusing on the interface between ABC and CBA rhombohedral sequences, modeled via a 1D SSH-like chain Hamiltonian.
key_observables: Energy bands, density of states (DOS), and charge density distribution.
important_parameters: Interlayer separations (3.25-3.53 A), K and K' points of the Brillouin zone, and the topological condition |qa| < sqrt(2/3) |tz/t|.
main_assumptions: The system is modeled using nearest-neighbor intralayer and interlayer hopping; the SSH mapping is used for physical interpretation.
figures_summary: Fig 1 shows flat bands in an ABC slab; Fig 2 shows charge localization in Bernal/rhombohedral interfaces; Fig 3 shows four flat bands in the inverted ABC/CBA interface; Fig 4 illustrates the stacking inversion structure.
paper_structure: The paper begins by investigating rhombohedral slabs, moves to Bernal/rhombohedral interfaces, then presents the central result of inverted rhombohedral interfaces, supported by an SSH model mapping.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
